# Copilot Instructions — copilot-governance

## Purpose and Core Problem

This repo solves **cross-platform, cross-machine AI governance**: ensuring that VS Code Copilot, Claude Code, and Google Antigravity on any laptop (currently Chromebook/Crostini as primary) all receive the same global instructions, skills, agents, and lifecycle hooks — without duplication or manual per-machine setup.

**The strategic challenge:** Three different IDE platforms each have different global-config formats and install paths. This repo must package global-items so they are **easily installed once and auto-updating** on every new machine.

---

## Platform Landscape (April 2026)

| Platform | Global Config Root | Skills Path | Rules/Instructions | Hooks |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **VS Code Copilot** | `~/.copilot/` (via settings) | `~/.copilot/skills/` | `~/.copilot/instructions/` | `~/.copilot/hooks/global-standards.json` |
| **Claude Code** | `~/.claude/` | `~/.claude/skills/` (also reads `.agents/skills/`) | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `~/.claude/rules/` | `~/.claude/settings.json` → `hooks` key |
| **Google Antigravity** | `~/.gemini/` | `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/` | `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md`, `.agents/rules/` | Not yet documented |

**Key insight:** All three platforms consume the same `SKILL.md` format from the [agentskills.io open standard](https://agentskills.io/). Skills in `skills/*/SKILL.md` already work in VS Code Copilot, Claude Code, and Antigravity without modification.

**Google Antigravity** (`antigravity.google`) is Google's local, agent-first IDE — the successor to Firebase Studio (sunset March 2027). It runs locally on Linux/macOS/Windows, supports Claude/Gemini/GPT models, and uses `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` for global rules and `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/` for global skills. Available for Linux (glibc ≥ 2.28), meaning it runs in Crostini on Chromebook.

---

## Current Architecture

```
install.sh           → idempotent bootstrap: symlink ~/.copilot/, merge VS Code settings.json
instructions/        → 7 always-on .instructions.md files (VS Code Copilot only)
skills/              → 18+ SKILL.md files — agentskills.io format, works in all 3 platforms
agents/              → 4 .agent.md files (VS Code Copilot + Claude Code format)
hooks/
  global-standards.json  → hook event wiring for VS Code Copilot
  scripts/*.py           → SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop lifecycle scripts
```

VS Code loads via settings: `chat.instructionsFilesLocations`, `chat.agentSkillsLocations`, `chat.agentFilesLocations`, `chat.hookFilesLocations` → all pointing to `~/.copilot/`.

---

## Unified Architecture (Cross-Platform, Conflict-Free)

**No extension system. No marketplace. One architecture for all platforms.**

Both VS Code and Antigravity use identical install mechanics: a git clone at `~/copilot-governance/` with symlinks pointing each IDE into it. The repo IS the config source — no copying, no translation layer. Claude Code works the same way.

### Platform Config Wiring

| Platform | Wired By | What Points Into the Repo |
|---|---|---|
| **VS Code Copilot** | `~/.copilot/ → ~/copilot-governance/` symlink + `settings.json` keys | `skills/`, `instructions/`, `agents/`, `hooks/` |
| **Google Antigravity** | `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` `@`-imports + `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/ → ~/copilot-governance/skills/` symlink | All instructions via `GEMINI.md`; skills via symlink |
| **Claude Code** | `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` `@`-imports + `~/.claude/skills/ → ~/copilot-governance/skills/` symlink | All instructions via `CLAUDE.md`; skills via symlink |

**Antigravity `@`-import syntax** (rules natively support absolute-path imports):
```markdown
# ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md — generated by install.sh; do not edit manually
@/home/USERNAME/copilot-governance/instructions/global-standards.instructions.md
@/home/USERNAME/copilot-governance/instructions/github-governance.instructions.md
# ... one line per instructions file
```

The repo root also contains `GEMINI.md` and `CLAUDE.md` as template files — tracked in git with a `${REPO_DIR}` placeholder. `install.sh` runs `envsubst` to produce the real `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` and `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` with the correct absolute path for that machine.

---

## Git Sync Architecture: PR-Based Approval

GitHub's standard PR model provides **both conflict prevention and user approval** in one mechanism. No machine branches. No push daemon. No auto-merge bot.

```
GitHub (main — protected, merges require PR + approval)
     ↑ self-annealing: AI creates PR → user approves → merges to main
     ↓ pull timer (every 15 min, silent, all machines)

Machine A (VS Code)     Machine B (VS Code)     Machine C (Antigravity)
  pulls ← main            pulls ← main            pulls ← main
  creates PRs ↑           creates PRs ↑           creates PRs ↑
```

**Self-annealing flow — when a machine improves a global-item:**
1. AI identifies an efficiency gain in a skill or instruction
2. AI edits the file and invokes `sync/create-pr.sh --skill <name> --title "..." --body "..."`
3. Script creates `feat/anneal-<skill>-<date>` branch, commits, pushes, opens a **draft PR**
4. User receives a GitHub notification and reviews the specific change
5. User approves (or edits and approves) and merges to `main`
6. PR is the approval gate — nothing reaches other machines without it

**Pull flow — how approved improvements reach all machines:**
- systemd user timer runs `sync/pull.sh` every 15 minutes on every machine
- `git merge --ff-only origin/main` silently delivers all merged PRs
- Symlinks mean skills and instructions are live immediately — no IDE restart
- Conflicts cannot occur because machines never push directly to `main`

**Why not a push daemon?**
A blind push daemon auto-commits all dirty state and bypasses review. Self-annealing improvements must be discrete, named, and user-approved. The PR is the commit — not a background cron job.

### Automation Components

**Pull timer** — `sync/governance-pull.service` + `sync/governance-pull.timer` (systemd user, every 15 min):
```bash
# sync/pull.sh — runs automatically; safe to invoke manually
cd ~/copilot-governance
git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main
```

**PR creation** — `sync/create-pr.sh` (invoked by the AI after self-annealing):
```bash
# Creates a branch, commits, pushes, and opens a draft PR for user review.
sync/create-pr.sh \
  --skill github-ops-excellence \
  --title "Improve merge queue detection for draft PRs" \
  --body "Root cause: ... Change: ... Evidence: ..."
```
Requires `gh` CLI installed and authenticated (`gh auth login`, once per machine).

**CI validation** — `.github/workflows/validate-pr.yml`:
- Runs on every PR targeting `main`
- Validates SKILL.md frontmatter, non-empty instruction files, no secret patterns
- Must pass before the user can approve

### Bootstrap (Identical for All Platforms)

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chf3198/copilot-governance/main/install.sh | bash
```

`install.sh` (idempotent, all platforms in one pass):
1. Clone repo to `~/copilot-governance` (skip if already present)
2. Symlink `~/.copilot/ → ~/copilot-governance/`, merge VS Code `settings.json`
3. Run `envsubst` on `GEMINI.md` template → `~/.gemini/GEMINI.md` with real paths
4. Symlink `~/.gemini/antigravity/skills/ → ~/copilot-governance/skills/`
5. Run `envsubst` on `CLAUDE.md` template → `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` with real paths
6. Symlink `~/.claude/skills/ → ~/copilot-governance/skills/`
7. Install systemd user timer (`governance-pull.timer`) and enable it
8. Enable lingering (`loginctl enable-linger`) so timer survives logout

### On Any Machine: Force Sync or Create a PR

```bash
# Force immediate pull of approved changes from main:
bash ~/copilot-governance/sync/pull.sh

# Propose a self-annealed improvement for user approval:
bash ~/copilot-governance/sync/create-pr.sh \
  --skill <skill-folder-name> \
  --title "<what improved, imperative, ≤72 chars>" \
  --body "<why and evidence>"
```

---

## GitHub Repo Structure

```
install.sh                → idempotent bootstrap for all platforms + sync setup
GEMINI.md                 → Antigravity rules template (${REPO_DIR} placeholder; install.sh generates ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md)
CLAUDE.md                 → Claude Code instructions template (${REPO_DIR} placeholder; install.sh generates ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md)
instructions/             → 8 always-on .instructions.md files (VS Code + imported by others)
skills/                   → 18+ SKILL.md files — agentskills.io format, all 3 platforms
agents/                   → 4 .agent.md files (VS Code + Claude Code)
hooks/                    → VS Code hook wiring + lifecycle scripts
sync/
  pull.sh                 → pull from main (invoked by systemd timer)
  create-pr.sh            → create a draft PR for a self-annealed improvement (invoked by AI)
  governance-pull.timer   → systemd user timer unit (every 15 min)
  governance-pull.service → systemd user service for pull
.github/
  workflows/
    validate-pr.yml       → CI: validate SKILL.md format + no secrets on PRs to main
  copilot-instructions.md → this file
```

Do **not** split into multiple repos. One repo, one `git pull`, all platforms updated. Standard GitHub fork/PR model for public collaboration — no special branch conventions needed.

---

## Install / Update Workflow

```bash
# New machine — any platform (VS Code, Antigravity, Claude Code)
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chf3198/copilot-governance/main/install.sh | bash

# One-time: authenticate gh CLI so PRs can be created from this machine
gh auth login

# Pull approved improvements on demand (runs automatically every 15 min):
bash ~/copilot-governance/sync/pull.sh

# Propose a self-annealed improvement from this machine:
bash ~/copilot-governance/sync/create-pr.sh \
  --skill <skill-folder-name> \
  --title "<imperative description ≤72 chars>" \
  --body "<root cause, change made, evidence>"
```

Symlinks pick up skill and instruction changes immediately after a pull — no reinstall, no IDE restart required.

---

## Role Execution Model

All non-trivial tasks use a **single-thread baton handoff**:  
`Manager (scope + gates) → Collaborator (implement) → Admin (git/release ops) → Consultant (critique)`

- At most one role active at a time. Emit a short handoff artifact at each transition.
- Skills map to roles: `role-manager-execution`, `role-collaborator-execution`, `role-admin-execution`, `role-consultant-critique`. Orchestrate via `role-baton-orchestrator`.
- The **user is the client** — consulted only for design decisions and UAT sign-off. Never ask the user to perform manual steps.

## Skill Invocation Pattern

Skills are loaded on demand by name reference. Invoke by naming the skill:

```
invoke `github-ticket-lifecycle-orchestrator` skill
invoke `release-version-integrity` skill
invoke `workflow-self-anneal` skill
```

Each `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` is the source of truth for that domain. Some skills include an `INSTALL-GLOBAL.md` for initial setup steps.

---

## GitHub Governance Conventions

- **Issue titles**: plain imperative ≤72 chars — no `type(scope):` prefix, no `[TAG]` brackets.
- **Commit/PR titles**: Conventional Commits — `type(scope): description`. Types: `feat fix chore content perf refactor docs style test`.
- **Branch naming**: `<type>/<issue-number>-<short-slug>` (e.g. `fix/5-nav-contrast`).
- Every change requires a linked issue with `type:*` label, priority, milestone, and project assignment before coding starts.
- Route all GitHub workflow questions through `github-ops-tree-router` → specialist skill.

---

## Hook Behavior

| Hook | Script | What It Does |
|---|---|---|
| `SessionStart` | `session_context.py` | Injects machine/session context, detects repo signals and community health gaps |
| `PreToolUse` | `pretool_guard.py` | Blocks dangerous commands (`rm -rf /`, `mkfs`, etc.) and operations on secret-bearing files (`.env`, `*.pem`, `*.key`) |
| `PostToolUse` | `posttool_reminders.py` | Triggers governance reminders after tool calls |
| `Stop` | `stop_reminder.py` | Gate check before session close |

Hook scripts read a JSON payload from stdin and emit a JSON response. When porting to the Agent Plugin format, hook commands must use `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` instead of hardcoded `~/.copilot/` paths.

---

## Post-Merge Checklist (Mandatory)

After every PR merge or deployment changing user-facing behavior, address all six gates before marking complete:

1. **CHANGELOG** — entry for every shipped behavioral change
2. **README sync** — update if user-visible behavior changed
3. **Profile governance** — run `repo-profile-governance` skill (community health files, metadata, templates)
4. **Docs drift** — run `docs-drift-maintenance` skill
5. **Learnings** — add to `docs/workflow/learnings.md` if a significant discovery was made
6. **Release integrity** — run `release-version-integrity` if package/extension behavior changed

---

## Key Constraints

- Never expose secrets in repo files, logs, or examples — use placeholders.
- Prefer root-cause fixes over band-aids.
- Never claim build/test/release success without explicit evidence.
- Changes must be minimal, localized, and reversible.
- Docs updates ship **with** behavior changes — never as follow-up.
- Skills must remain in `agentskills.io`-compliant SKILL.md format to preserve cross-platform compatibility.
